title: Vitis mutabilis
taxonomy: Vitis mutabilis is classified under the genus Vitis, which includes various species of grapevines. This species is native to China and is often referred to as the Chinese grapevine. It is a member of the Vitaceae family, which comprises over 10 genera of grapevines and other related plants. As part of the Vitaceae family, Vitis mutabilis is closely related to other species of grapes and grapevines. The classification of Vitis mutabilis provides insight into its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

benefits: Vitis mutabilis, also known as the Chinese Mutabilis grape, is said to offer various health benefits due to its rich nutritional content. preparations: Vitis mutabilis is also known as Chinese Bastard Tea or Butterfly Vine. It has been used in traditional medicine in China for various purposes. The plant's herbal preparations are primarily used for treating fever, cough, and rheumatism. The leaves of Vitis mutabilis are used to make teas and infusions, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of its herbal preparations.
intro: Vitis mutabilis, also known as the Chinese grapevine or Chinese mutabilis grape, is a species of flowering plant in the grapevine family (Vitaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities that contribute to its use in treating various health conditions, such as fever, diarrhea, and skin issues. The bioactive constituents of Vitis mutabilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Vitis mutabilis is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used to make medicinal wines and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
